name: phonetic_ipa_english
reference: English IPA phonetic symbols rendered with standard English-Braille letter / digraph values
_note: International Phonetic Alphabet (English) symbol -> braille cell sequence. Universal / scheme-neutral, so it lives at the resources root beside latin/ greek/ music/, not under any region: the cell values follow the standard English-Braille letter and digraph conventions (p = dots-1234, θ = dots-1456 "th", ʃ = dots-156 "sh", ŋ = dots-1246 "ng"), independent of any regional Chinese-braille scheme, which is why cn_current and cn_ncb reference this one shared table identically. Each key is the IPA string a user types between phonetic delimiters (e.g. "iː", "tʃ", "eɪ"); values reference named cells (c_*) from cells.json. Multi-character symbols (long vowels iː, diphthongs eɪ, affricates tʃ) are stored whole and matched greedily longest-first by the backend, so a bare "t" and the affricate "tʃ" stay distinct. Both the IPA script g (ɡ, U+0261) and the ASCII g (U+0067) map to the same cell so either keystroke works. Stress marks (ˈ ˌ) are intentionally absent — they have no cell in this table and the backend flags them rather than inventing braille.
